steps.title: Distribute 3xy to both terms
steps.content:
Multiply $3xy$ by each term inside the parentheses:
$$3xy(2x^{2}+7y) = (3xy) \cdot 2x^{2} + (3xy) \cdot 7y$$

steps.title: Simplify each product
steps.content:
$3xy \cdot 2x^{2} = 6x^{3}y$ (multiply coefficients: $3 \cdot 2 = 6$, and combine $x \cdot x^{2} = x^{3}$).
$3xy \cdot 7y = 21xy^{2}$ (multiply coefficients: $3 \cdot 7 = 21$, and combine $y \cdot y = y^{2}$).

Combined: $$6x^{3}y + 21xy^{2}$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
